title: Two simultaneous typhoons hit Japan with record rainfall and landslide warnings
summary: The passage of typhoons 7 and 8 left intense rainfall, massive evacuations and damage in several regions of the country.

title: Strong magnitude 6 earthquake in Yamanashi causes injuries and concern about Mount Fuji
summary: The earthquake, the strongest in 102 years in the region, left at least 21 injured and ruled out any relationship with volcanic activity.

title: Small plane crashes into skyscraper in Beijing: one dead and 13 injured
summary: Authorities took a day to report the accident, which occurred in an office building in the Chinese capital.

title: China and Russia conduct joint air patrol over the Sea of Japan
summary: The air forces of both countries demonstrated their strategic cooperation with flights in the region, in a message of deterrence.

title: Writer Shouji Kaoru, author of 'Beware of the Red Scarf Girl', dies
summary: The novelist, aged 88, was one of the most read authors in Japan in the 1970s.

title: United States restricts access to advanced artificial intelligence and OpenAI limits its new model
summary: The Trump administration authorizes the 'Mythos' model only for trusted companies, while OpenAI criticizes the measure.

title: Cape Verde makes history by qualifying for the World Cup round of 16 for the first time
summary: The African team advanced with three draws in the group stage, standing out for their defensive solidity.

title: Two men drown in a river in Ehime while practicing stand-up paddleboarding during the typhoon
summary: The victims were swept away by the current in the Kamo River, possibly due to the increased flow caused by the rains.

title: A landslide in Yamaguchi causes the death of a 70-year-old man
summary: Heavy rains caused the earth to collapse onto a house in Heiwa, also leaving three injured.

title: Spain defeats Uruguay and advances as group leader in the World Cup
summary: The Spanish team showed precise passing to overcome Uruguay, while the Uruguayan goalkeeper made a key mistake.

title: Japan prepares to face Brazil in a decisive match for a spot in the round of 16
summary: The Japanese players express confidence and respect for the five-time champions, with a focus on concentration and teamwork.

title: Egypt and Iran face off in a match marked by political protests
summary: The match, considered a 'pride match', is played amid diplomatic tensions and social demands.

title: Taiwan's Legislative Yuan president meets with US counterpart in Washington
summary: Both leaders discussed security and economic cooperation issues, in a gesture that reinforces ties between Taiwan and the United States.

title: Mayor elected by a one-vote margin challenges annulment of his victory in Nasu
summary: The mayor of the city in Tochigi filed a lawsuit to overturn the decision that declared his election invalid by a minimal margin.

title: 32 years since the sarin gas attack in Matsumoto: tributes and reflections on media coverage
summary: Residents and media remember the tragic event that marked Japan, with calls for ethics in judicial reporting.

title: An agricultural pond breaks in Hiroshima and floods several homes
summary: Torrential rains caused the collapse of the levee, causing flooding in at least ten houses with no casualties.

title: Baseball roundup: key wins, home runs and changes in Major League Baseball
summary: Notable results include Okamoto's home run, Sasaki's loss, and the firing of the Angels' general manager.

title: Japan faces challenges in integrating foreign workers and combating discrimination
summary: A teacher of Brazilian descent seeks to inspire children to keep their dreams, while the debate on immigrant acceptance grows.

title: Takeda names US executive of South Korean descent as new president
summary: The Japanese pharmaceutical company bets on a global leader to boost its strategy in key therapeutic areas.

title: Proposed reform of imperial succession law allows transmission of the throne through male line of collateral families
summary: The amendment bill establishes that adopted male children from former imperial houses could accede to the throne.
